raw_question: Among adults with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease, what structured data elements would be needed in an EHR or claims dataset to study initiation of an SGLT2 inhibitor?

pico.population: Adults 18 years or older with type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) and chronic kidney disease (CKD stages 1–5, excluding ESRD) in an EHR or administrative claims database
pico.population_detail: Baseline window of 12 months before the first qualifying SGLT2 prescription (index date); continuous enrollment or care engagement required
pico.intervention: Initiation of an SGLT2 inhibitor (new-user design: no SGLT2 prescription in the 12-month baseline period before index date)
outcomes: Availability and completeness of T2DM diagnosis codes (ICD-10-CM E11.*) in structured data
Availability of CKD diagnosis codes (ICD-10-CM N18.*) with stage specificity
Documentation of baseline eGFR laboratory value (LOINC 62238-1 or 33914-3) within 90 days before index
Documentation of baseline UACR (LOINC 14959-1) within 180 days before index
Completeness of SGLT2 prescription records (drug name, date, days supply) in pharmacy or MedicationRequest data
Availability of comorbidity codes (cardiovascular disease, heart failure, neuropathy) in structured fields
Availability of age, sex, and race/ethnicity in demographic data
pico.timeframe: Baseline period: 12 months before the index date (first SGLT2 prescription). Data element completeness is assessed during this window.
pico.study_intent: Data readiness assessment — enumerate and evaluate the structured data elements required to operationalize a new-user SGLT2 inhibitor cohort study in a real-world dataset

ambiguity_flags.description: Data source type is not specified — EHR vs. administrative claims vs. linked data affect which data elements are available and how complete they are
ambiguity_flags.suggested_clarification: Is the target data source an EHR (e.g., Epic, Cerner), a claims database (e.g., MarketScan, Optum), or a linked EHR-claims dataset?

ambiguity_flags.description: The new-user washout period is not specified. A 12-month baseline is standard but some studies use 6 months or require continuous enrollment
ambiguity_flags.suggested_clarification: What lookback window defines 'new user' — 6 months, 12 months, or enrollment duration?

ambiguity_flags.description: It is unclear whether data completeness thresholds are required (e.g., 80% of patients must have a baseline eGFR) or whether the assessment is purely descriptive
ambiguity_flags.suggested_clarification: Is this a go/no-go feasibility check with defined completeness thresholds, or a descriptive characterization of data availability?

ambiguity_flags.description: Continuous enrollment definition varies by data source — some claims databases require 6–12 months of coverage; EHRs may use care engagement proxies
ambiguity_flags.suggested_clarification: What minimum enrollment or care engagement period is required in the baseline period?

clarifying_questions: Is the target data source an EHR system, administrative claims database, or a linked EHR-claims dataset?
What is the minimum required data completeness for each data element (e.g., at least 80% of the cohort must have a baseline eGFR)?
What lookback window defines a new user of SGLT2 inhibitors — 6 months, 12 months, or enrollment duration?
Should race and ethnicity be required fields, or treated as optional due to known missingness in clinical data?
Should provider specialty (e.g., nephrology, endocrinology) be captured as a data element to characterize care setting?
Are procedure codes (CPT) required for dialysis exclusion, or is ICD-10-CM code N18.6 sufficient?

required: SGLT2 prescription record (drug name, date, days supply)
optional: Days supply and refill data for medication adherence
